name: Miro API
what_it_does: Miro is an online collaborative whiteboard platform. Its REST API v2 enables agents to create and manage boards, add visual items (sticky notes, shapes, connectors, images, text, cards, frames), and automate whiteboard content. WebSocket support enables real-time board updates.
use_cases: Generating visual diagrams or architecture charts from structured agent data
Populating retrospective or brainstorming boards with agent-processed input
Creating and organizing sticky notes from meeting transcripts or analysis
Building real-time monitoring dashboards on Miro boards
Automating mind maps or flowcharts from data sources
not_for: Data storage or retrieval (Miro is a visual canvas, not a database)
Agents needing sub-second write latency at high throughput
Document generation where structured output format matters over visual layout
best_when: An agent generates visual content that humans will collaborate on — diagrams, boards, or structured visual representations of complex data.
avoid_when: Your agent's output is primarily textual or tabular — a doc or spreadsheet tool is more appropriate.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 required for all API access. Scopes are granular: boards:read, boards:write, microphone:listen, etc. Access tokens are short-lived with refresh tokens. No API key option for agents — OAuth user context is always required. Miro Apps (installed to workspaces) can use service account tokens for agent automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 3 editable boards, unlimited viewers, limited templates
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $8/member/mo (unlimited boards); Business $16/member/mo (advanced features); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier's 3-board limit is a hard constraint for agents that create boards dynamically. Starter plan required for most agent automation scenarios. API access is available on all tiers.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests/second per OAuth token; 300 items per create-items-in-bulk call

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are well-structured JSON with type, message, and context fields. HTTP status codes are used correctly. Rate limit errors include Retry-After headers. Some item-type-specific validation errors could be more descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Item creation always creates new items. Agents must track item IDs locally or query board state before writing to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth-only auth requires initial user authorization — no simple API key for headless agents
Free tier 3-board limit is quickly exhausted by agents that create boards per-task
Item positions on the board use an x/y coordinate system — agents must manage layout explicitly
No idempotency — item creation is always additive; implement state tracking to avoid duplicates
Bulk operations are capped at 300 items per request — large boards require batching
WebSocket connections for real-time updates require separate auth and connection management
Board content queries are paginated but can be slow for large boards with thousands of items

score_rationale: Miro's API v2 is one of the better visual collaboration APIs — good documentation, an OpenAPI spec, official SDKs in multiple languages, WebSocket support, granular OAuth scopes, and solid error messages. The main friction points for agents are OAuth-only auth (no API keys), no idempotency, the free tier board limit, and the need to manage x/y layout coordinates explicitly. Strong choice for agents that generate visual content humans will collaborate on.
